title: Space Force seeks fresh bidders for satellite-control antennas
summary: The U.S. Space Force is reopening bids for satellite-control antennas after canceling a $1.7 billion contract. The initiative, named SCAR, aims to restart the procurement process for these critical assets.

title: Shield Space and ClearSpace partner to defend satellites from orbital threats
summary: Shield Space and ClearSpace have partnered to enhance satellite defense against orbital threats by integrating Shield Space's autonomous satellite operations software with ClearSpace's in-orbit servicing capabilities.

title: MDA Space wins contract for Radarsat replenishment satellite
summary: MDA Space has secured a contract to build a radar imaging satellite for the Canadian Space Agency. The satellite will serve as a replenishment for the existing Radarsat system while the government explores next-generation options.

title: Vast signs additional partners for commercial space station microgravity research
summary: Vast has expanded its network of partners for microgravity research and manufacturing on its commercial space station. The announcement was made on June 24.

title: York satellite demonstrates two-way UHF communications from low Earth orbit
summary: York Space Systems announced that a satellite built for the U.S. Space Force successfully demonstrated two-way tactical communications using UHF links in low Earth orbit. This achievement highlights advancements in secure and efficient satellite communication technologies.

title: How space weather could bust the AI boom
summary: The rapid expansion of AI data centers is straining America's electric grid, and space weather events could exacerbate these challenges. This highlights the vulnerability of critical infrastructure to both terrestrial and space-based disruptions.

title: Vantor selects BAE Systems to build next-generation imaging satellites
summary: Vantor has selected BAE Systems to develop its next-generation high-resolution imaging satellites. This partnership reunites Vantor with the former Ball Aerospace team, which previously contributed to DigitalGlobe's satellite programs.

title: SpaceX launches secretive Starfall reentry demo mission
summary: SpaceX conducted the first test flight of its secretive Starfall reentry capsule on June 23. The mission's details remained largely undisclosed.

title: NGSO trade association launches without industry giant SpaceX
summary: A new trade association for non-geostationary satellite operators has been launched, with Amazon and other companies participating, but SpaceX, the largest NGSO constellation operator, is notably absent.

title: OHB raises funding for expansion, acquisitions
summary: OHB, a German space company, plans to raise approximately half a billion euros through a stock sale to expand its facilities and pursue acquisitions.

title: NASA's aging infrastructure can't handle Artemis launches without $1 billion in upgrades, watchdog warns
summary: NASA's Artemis program requires $1 billion in infrastructure upgrades to handle upcoming launches, according to a watchdog report. The agency's aging facilities are struggling to support the growing demands of lunar missions and the commercial space sector.

title: This is the largest and most detailed image of our Milky Way — with over 60 million stars and 50 exoplanet systems
summary: ESA's Euclid spacecraft has captured the largest and most detailed image of the Milky Way's heart, featuring over 60 million stars and 50 exoplanet systems. This marks a significant achievement in space observation and exoplanet research.

title: '50% superhero and 50% noir, but 100% totally new': 'Spider-Noir' composers  on injecting retro style into the superhero multiverse (interview)
summary: The interview discusses the creative process behind the soundtrack for 'Spider-Noir', blending retro noir and superhero styles. Composers highlight a supportive environment that encouraged bold, unconventional ideas.

title: Did NASA just find evidence of ancient life on Mars? Perseverance rover spots complex carbon in Red Planet rocks
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover has detected complex carbon compounds in Martian rocks, the most robust organic findings in Jezero Crater. This discovery raises questions about potential ancient life on Mars.

title: SpaceX launching 24 Starlink satellites from California tonight: Watch it live
summary: SpaceX is set to launch 24 Starlink satellites aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base on June 24. The event can be watched live.

title: NASA sent a FIFA World Cup 2026 ball to the ISS so astronauts can test their footwork in zero gravity (video)
summary: NASA sent a FIFA World Cup 2026 ball to the International Space Station (ISS) for astronauts to test their footwork in zero gravity. The experiment aims to assist in FIFA ball design while providing entertainment for the ISS crew.

title: As 'Avatar: Fire & Ash' hits Disney+, we look at 3 of the weirdest alien creatures in the 'Avatar' movies
summary: The release of 'Avatar: Fire & Ash' on Disney+ highlights three bizarre alien creatures from the Avatar movies. The article explores these unique creatures inhabiting the jungles of Pandora.

title: “Seeing this Nikon Z8 deal is painful — especially since I bought mine at full price!” Our Senior Manager took the hit so you don’t have to!
summary: A Nikon Z8 camera deal at Walmart's lowest-ever price was highlighted, with a humorous tone about a senior manager's sacrifice to share the deal. The focus was on astrophotography use for the camera.

title: NASA looks for the origins of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS | Space photo of the day for June 24, 2026
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope is uncovering clues about the origins of the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS. This discovery could provide insights into the composition and behavior of objects from outside our solar system.

title: NASA’s TESS Mission Reveals the “Puffiest” Planets Ever Found
summary: NASA's TESS mission discovered two new 'super-puff' planets with densities comparable to cotton candy. These Jupiter-sized worlds are the puffiest planets ever identified.

title: NASA at the Ion: Orion Lessons from Artemis II Shape NASA’s Moon to Mars Path
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft returned four astronauts from Artemis II, humanity's first crewed journey around the Moon since Apollo. The mission's achievements are shaping NASA's plans for future lunar and Mars missions.

title: La NASA compartirá los últimos avances del programa Base Lunar
summary: NASA will share the latest updates on the Lunar Base Lea program in a virtual conversation led by administrator Jared Isaacman on June 30. The event will highlight NASA's plans for constructing a lunar surface base.

title: NASA to Share Latest Moon Base Mission Progress
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will host a virtual update on June 30 to discuss progress on the agency's Moon Base mission plans, including new lunar lander mission awards. The conversation will also cover upcoming developments for establishing a sustainable lunar presence.

title: Roman Telescope Comes to Kennedy
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope arrived at Kennedy Space Center in Florida on June 21, 2026, for launch preparations. The telescope is set to launch no earlier than August 30, 2026.

title: NASA’s HiRISE Captures Perseverance Marking a Milestone on Mars
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover has been captured by the HiRISE camera on Mars, marking a milestone after traveling a full marathon distance (26.2 miles) on the Red Planet. The rover achieved this feat after five years and four months of exploration.

title: Euclid View of Milky Way Heart Previews Core Survey by NASA’s Roman
summary: Euclid, an ESA mission with NASA contributions, has captured a new view of the Milky Way's heart, previewing a region that NASA's Roman Space Telescope will observe after its upcoming launch. This early data provides astronomers with a head start on a key Roman survey.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4927–4933: Let’s Drive to That Smooth Area
summary: The Curiosity rover's science team is planning to drive to a smoother area based on orbital images showing varied surface textures. The blog is authored by a planetary mineralogy professor at The Open University in the UK.

title: Playing the Moon Game
summary: Apollo astronauts trained in Alaska's Valley of Ten Thousand Smokes to prepare for their roles as lunar field geologists. This mission rehearsal simulated Moon-like geological conditions on Earth.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launch schedule features six orbital launches, including the first Pegasus XL mission. The Pegasus XL will share the manifest with Falcon 9 and Chinese missions.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle on Tuesday morning using a Falcon 9 rocket. This test marks a significant step in the development of SpaceX's reentry technology.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is preparing for repairs to the Canadarm2 robotic arm as the CRS-34 cargo mission departs. The repairs are part of ongoing maintenance following recent operational issues.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space has transported the second stage of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket to Stennis Space Center, marking progress toward a planned 2026 debut. The company continues advancing development of the fully reusable vehicle.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled from various global sites in the upcoming week, indicating a busy period for the space industry. This launch manifest highlights the high activity across multiple spaceports worldwide.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is headquartered in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has successfully completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is approaching a static fire test. This marks continued progress in SpaceX's development efforts.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China launched its tallest rocket to date as part of a busy month with three missions for a single internet constellation. LandSpace is preparing for a second landing attempt.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for Japan's space program after a previous failure.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but the specific details were not provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events will inform industry about ESA's planned activities, priorities, and opportunities.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for versatile applications in the space industry. This innovation could significantly enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association, a new trade group for non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite operators. This collaboration aims to advocate for policies and industry interests in the growing NGSO sector.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a new era in the reentry sector. The event validates emerging startups in the field.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
summary: NASA's launch facilities are expected to reach capacity by the end of the decade due to a surge in commercial launches. A report from the agency's inspector general highlights this impending challenge.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space raised $7 million and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demo flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit.

title: Ubotica Raises $11M for Satellite-Based AI Tech
summary: Ubotica, a Dublin-based space AI company, raised $11 million to advance its AI-powered intelligence platform for maritime security using satellite technology.

title: A Faster Path to Orbit: How Hosted Payloads Are Changing Access to Space
summary: Hosted payloads are revolutionizing space access by offering a faster and more economical alternative to traditional methods. Companies no longer need to build everything from scratch, reducing time and investment.

title: Exclusive: Northwood Unveils New Antenna, Expanded Network Capacity
summary: Northwood has unveiled a new antenna to expand its network capacity, enabling more data downlinks across a wider range of frequencies. This development supports the company's rapid global expansion of its ground station footprint.

title: ElevationSpace Closes $40M Series B
summary: Japanese satellite reentry startup ElevationSpace raised $40 million in a Series B funding round, bringing its total funding to $63.5 million.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated high-fidelity quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its quantum networking satellite, Q4S, ahead of a planned 2027 flight. The company aims to advance quantum communication technology through this milestone.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3 with its autonomous orbital vehicle Jackal, meeting all test objectives and achieving full commissioning.

title: BAE Systems to Build Buses for Vantor’s Vantage Imaging Satellites
summary: BAE Systems will construct the buses for Vantor's Vantage imaging satellites, with the first two satellites expected to become operational by 2029.

title: Space Force Awards Boeing $2 Billion MUOS Service Life Extension Contract
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Boeing a $2 billion contract to extend the service life of the Mobile User Objective System (MUOS) with two new satellites. The MUOS system, deployed in 2019, provides secure communications for military operations.

title: Amazon Leo to Connect Construction Equipment for Hitachi Construction Machinery
summary: Amazon Leo has partnered with Hitachi Construction Machinery to connect its construction equipment as part of a service preview for enterprises. This collaboration expands Amazon Leo's reach into the construction industry.

title: The EU’s 2 GHz Plan: A Test Case for Sovereignty in Satcom
summary: The European Commission proposed re-authorizing the 2 GHz Mobile Satellite Services band beyond 2027, highlighting satellite connectivity's geopolitical significance. This move is seen as a test case for EU sovereignty in satellite communications.

title: LEO Operators Launch New Industry Group for NGSO Issues
summary: A new trade association called SpaceConnect Association was launched by Amazon, Iridium, Telesat, and Globalstar to represent Non-Geostationary (NGSO) satellite issues. This group aims to address industry challenges and advocate for NGSO satellite operators.

title: Spanish IoT Startup FOSSA Secures $10M in Funding
summary: Spanish IoT satellite startup FOSSA raised $10.5 million to expand its sovereign satellite capabilities and international growth. The funding will support global deployment of its satellite-based IoT solutions.

title: Northwood Space Debuts Parabolic Antenna, Designed for High-Volume Data
summary: Northwood Space introduced the Prism parabolic antenna, a 2.4-meter ground system designed for high-volume data connectivity. The new technology aims to enhance communication capabilities for space applications.

title: Space Sector Faces 400% Surge in Cyberattacks Amid Iran War, Experts Say
summary: The space sector has experienced a 400% increase in cyberattacks following U.S. and Israel-led military operations in Iran. Cybersecurity experts warn of a rise in both the tempo and sophistication of these attacks.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Selects Apex Space Bus for Orbital Compute Demo
summary: Sophia Space raised $7 million and selected Apex Space's Nova satellite bus for an orbital computing demonstration mission planned for 2027.

title: SpaceX Launches New Microgravity Lab Demo, Starfall
summary: SpaceX demonstrated a new reentry vehicle called Starfall for the first time, aimed at enabling regular access to microgravity for science and in-space manufacturing.
